No Poverty.reason: The course includes the topic "Leben ohne Geld" (Life without money), which tangentially relates to discussions on poverty and economic resources under Target 1.4.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops analytical and communication skills essential for employment and further learning (Target 4.4).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course improves skills for "職業上的應用" (professional applications) and uses themes like "Kommunikation und Beruf" (Communication and Profession), supporting skills for decent work (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The topic "Verkehr" (Traffic) is mentioned, which is a component of infrastructure (Target 9.1), but the focus is on writing skills, not infrastructure development.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Course themes include "Verkehr" (Traffic) and "Die Stadt im Fluss" (The city in flux), which relate to sustainable transport and urbanization (Targets 11.2, 11.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The theme "Leben ohne Geld" (Life without money) can be inferred to relate to discussions on alternative, sustainable lifestyles and consumption patterns (Target 12.8).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Practicing writing a "Leserbrief" (letter to the editor) is an exercise in public participation and freedom of expression, which are elements of Targets 16.7 and 16.10.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course aims to build "國際交流基本知能" (Abilities of global mobility), a foundational skill for international cooperation and knowledge sharing (Target 17.6).
